data.title: Market Sentiment Wanes as Clarity Act Faces Shrinking Senate Path
data.description:
The Clarity Act is facing a narrower path in the Senate after John Thune said he does not expect enough runway before the August recess, while the bill still needs 60 votes to advance. According to NS3.AI, Republicans released updated 616-page text covering ethics restrictions, but Democrats rejected the current draft.

Crypto trade groups have urged Senate leaders to begin floor consideration anyway. Analysts and prediction markets, meanwhile, pointed to slipping odds for the bill's progress.

data.title: Maverick Games Says Amazon Cutoff Has Not Hurt Business
data.description: Maverick Games, the studio behind the game Clutch, says being dropped by Amazon has not hurt its business, according to Bloomberg. The company said it is doing just fine after the cutoff.

data.title: Sui Launches Gas-Free Stablecoin Transfers
data.description: Sui said on X that it has launched a gas-free stablecoin transfer feature. According to Odaily, the function requires no gas fee or gas token and is built directly into the protocol.

data.title: Polymarket July 27 Israel-Iran Ceasefire Market Rises To 70.5%
data.description: The odds of a "yes" outcome in Polymarket's "Israel and Iran ceasefire continues" submarket for July 27 surged to 70.5% from 50.5% one hour earlier, a 20 percentage point jump. According to ChainCatcher, the sharp move reflects the impact of recent breaking news.

data.title: South Carolina Tapped For First 2028 Democratic Primary
data.description: According to Axios, a key Democratic National Committee Rules & Bylaws panel has preliminarily selected South Carolina to hold the first Democratic presidential primary of 2028, choosing it over Nevada in a vote that still needs full DNC ratification. The panel said South Carolina was elevated because it is a diverse, small state where candidates can compete without massive advertising budgets. The decision followed a contentious debate that split Black and Latino Democrats, as well as progressives and moderates. South Carolina Democrats and allies argued that Black voters would feel betrayed if the state were not first, while supporters of Nevada cited its large Latino and working-class electorate. Axios also reported that former Vice President Kamala Harris could benefit from the move because she is leading the potential 2028 Democratic field among Black voters. New Hampshire and possibly Iowa may still try to hold contests on their own, despite new Democratic rules aimed at punishing states that cut in line.

data.title: WISE Plans New Bank Application After OCC Rejection Under Stablecoin Framework
data.description: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency denied WISE’s application to establish a national trust bank. According to NS3.AI, WISE said it plans to submit a new application under the GENIUS Act’s stablecoin framework after the OCC cited anti-money laundering shortcomings.

data.title: Colombia's New Government Wins Historic $9 Billion CAF Offer
data.description:
CAF plans to provide President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella's administration with $9 billion in financing over the next four years, a record package for the Latin American development bank, according to Bloomberg.

The funding is expected to ease pressure on Colombia's strained public finances.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Threatens Higher Tariffs on the EU Over Fines on U.S. Companies
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said the European Union has targeted major U.S. companies with fines and that the U.S. will not allow it to continue. According to Jin10, he said the U.S. will immediately launch a Section 301 investigation, the fines will be completely revoked, and higher tariffs on the EU are expected as soon as possible.

data.title: Tesla Slumps, Booz Allen Surges on Earnings; Intel, Tenet, SAP Also Move on Results
data.description:
According to CNBC, shares of Tesla fell more than 3% Friday and were headed for a nearly 19% weekly decline after the company signaled higher artificial intelligence spending. Booz Allen Hamilton jumped 12% after fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.81 per share topped FactSet's $1.48 estimate, while Digital Realty Trust surged 15% after second-quarter earnings and revenue beat forecasts and the company raised full-year earnings guidance.

Other notable moves included C.H. Robinson Worldwide, which dropped 8% after a Dallas County, Texas jury issued an advisory verdict against the company and two other defendants in a trucking accident case involving an independent motor carrier, awarding $604 million in compensatory damages. Intel fell 4% even after reporting second-quarter revenue of $16.1 billion, up 25% from a year earlier, and adjusted earnings of 42 cents per share that beat expectations. Tenet Healthcare rose more than 16% after posting second-quarter adjusted earnings of $6.12 per share and revenue that both beat estimates, while SAP gained 8% after cloud backlog increased 27% year over year to 22.9 billion euros and revenue came in at 9.88 billion euros. American Express slipped 5% after missing second-quarter revenue estimates, and Verizon Communications rose 3% after reporting an earnings beat, a revenue miss and full-year guidance above expectations.

data.title: Crypto Advocacy Groups Urge Senate to Prioritize CLARITY Act Floor Vote
data.description: Three crypto advocacy groups have urged U.S. Senate leaders to prioritize floor consideration of the CLARITY Act, which needs 60 votes to pass. According to NS3.AI, the bill has already advanced through the Senate banking and agriculture committees, but some lawmakers said they plan to withhold support until key provisions are addressed.

data.title: Senate CLARITY Act Draft Draws Mixed Reactions Over Ethics Provision and Banking Concerns
data.description:
The latest Senate CLARITY Act draft has drawn mixed reactions after more than 600 pages of H.R. 3633 circulated by Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, Lummis and Moreno said they brokered an ethics provision agreement with the White House.

Democratic senators and some banking groups said the draft still has major shortcomings.

data.title: AI | Anthropic Rolls Out Lower-Cost Model for Workplace Tasks
data.description:
Anthropic PBC is rolling out a new artificial intelligence model designed to handle workplace tasks more affordably as customers grow more cost conscious and competition increases in China, Bloomberg reported.

The company is positioning the model as a lower-cost option for everyday work, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Federal Authorities Say Eight People Used Fake Checks to Steal $500,000 From New York Banks
data.description: Federal authorities accused eight people of using fake checks to steal $500,000 from New York banks. According to NS3.AI, the U.S. Department of Justice said the falsified checks totaled $3 million, but only $500,000 cleared before cash withdrawals were made.

data.title: ECB Chief Economist Lane: September Will Be the Next Key Policy Node
data.description:
ECB Chief Economist Philip R. Lane said the European Central Bank will reassess and possibly adjust its policy stance in September, with interest-rate decisions continuing to depend on economic data. According to Jin10, he said the current situation is a "medium-sized shock" and that whether policy is adjusted in September depends largely on whether oil and gas prices can stay high or whether a lasting solution can be found to reopen supply through the Strait of Hormuz.

Lane also said the European economy is mainly supported by domestic demand, adding that Europe trades with the whole world and that the United States is not a dominant factor in international trade. He said the ECB had previously kept rates unchanged to assess the impact of the Middle East conflict on eurozone inflation and economic growth.

data.title: PE Founder Pleads Guilty to $50 Million Investor Fraud
data.description:
A private equity firm founder and former Republican candidate for New Hampshire governor pleaded guilty to defrauding investors of more than $50 million, according to Bloomberg.

The case centers on allegations that the founder misled investors and took money from them in a fraud scheme.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Launches Opus 5 AI Model at Half the Price of Fable 5
data.description: According to Jin10, Anthropic announced the launch of its Opus 5 AI model, which it said comes close to the frontier intelligence level of Fable 5, but costs only half as much.

data.title: STOCKS | Qualcomm Shares Fall More Than 1.5% After Reportedly Notifying Customers of Double-Digit Price Increases
data.description: Qualcomm shares fell more than 1.5% after reports said it notified customers that prices will rise by a double-digit percentage. According to Jin10, the move came as消息称其通知客户价格将上涨两位数百分比.

data.title: Zilliqa Suspends Native Transactions After Ledger Signature Flaw Disclosure
data.description:
Zilliqa suspended native transactions after disclosing that roughly five affected Ledger app signatures from the same private key may allow attackers to reconstruct that key. According to NS3.AI, the flaw affects native, non-EVM transactions signed through Zilliqa Ledger app versions released between 2019 and 2026.

Zilliqa detected on-chain activity consistent with active exploitation on July 19 and did not quantify any losses. The network has not announced a reopening date or a final migration procedure.

data.title: Houthis Say Bab al-Mandeb Strait Is Not Blocked After Red Sea Shipping Threat
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing AFP, a Houthi spokesman said the group had not blocked traffic through the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait after announcing this week a maritime blockade on enemy states, including Saudi Arabia. The Iran-backed group said its position was limited to a sea blockade affecting the Saudi side only, in response to what it called Saudi Arabia's blockade of Houthi-controlled areas.

The statement came after the Houthis attacked a Saudi vessel in the nearby Red Sea. The group said on radio Monday that it would attack ships belonging to the enemy unless they complied with the blockade order. AFP said data it analyzed on Wednesday showed nine ships turned back after the Houthis announced the blockade, although some later resumed course.

The Houthis' attack has added to turbulence in global energy markets as clashes between Iran and the United States flare again. Brent crude briefly surged above USD 100 a barrel on Thursday. Separately, Kpler data cited by AFP showed that Saudi Arabia's seaborne crude exports through the Bab al-Mandeb Strait from March 2026 to mid-July 2026 were eight times higher than in the same period in 2025 after war broke out in the Middle East.

data.title: New Russian Oil Loading Halt Reported at Novorossiysk's Sheskharis Terminal
data.description: Oil loading at the Sheskharis oil terminal in Novorossiysk, Russia, has not taken place since Tuesday morning, July 21. According to Jin10, the terminal has remained without crude loading operations since then.
